observations: Round-1 HIGH (entry 17's whole-file shape unbounded) is CONFIRMED FIXED. Re-ran all three of this reviewer's own round-1 kill-shot fixtures (adjacent '7a.' line, duplicate contradictory line-7 with 'PASS (approved...)', trailing free-text 'Notes' section) verbatim against the exact revision-2 entry-17 command: all three now correctly FAIL, each on the new exactly-8-non-blank-lines check (each fixture has 9 lines). The duplicate-line-7 fixture would independently also fail the per-item-label-exactly-once check (label '7.' occurs twice), but the line-count check fires first in execution order -- both layers agree, no contradiction. The round-0 same-line-smuggled-prose kill-shot was also re-run and still correctly fails, now on the retained `grep -qxF` exact-line check (unchanged from revision 1).

Unsatisfiability check (the round-0 defect class) on the NEW SHA-256 pin: independently extracted the verbatim 8-line content from scope.files_create[0].purpose (by locating the '1. All existing framework validation green:' marker and taking everything from there to the end of the purpose string, via json.load -- never manually retyped), wrote it to a scratch file, and computed its SHA-256: `06f266c06abd9b3793477771de265698dbec0a00761c6d81818decbc7e31b5ea` -- an EXACT match to entry 17's pinned hash. The extracted content is independently confirmed to be exactly 8 non-blank lines, and items 7/8's text within it is byte-identical to the `grep -qxF` literals in the same entry. This proves the pin is achievable (not unsatisfiable) -- the round-0 failure mode (an acceptance test that can never pass for any implementation) does NOT recur here.

Content-honesty of pinned items 1-6 (041 precedent) independently re-verified against the real, current repository, read-only: item 1's text ('SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed' / 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed') exactly matches a live re-run of `bash tools/validate.sh` and `bash tools/validate.sh --payload`; item 2's claim that check C12 is part of that same sweep is confirmed by the live `[C12] PASS ecosystem-fixtures` line, correctly cited as a sub-fact of item 1 rather than needing its own separate evidence path (mirrors design_notes' 2<->entry-16-C12-line mapping); item 3's three cited directories (`.agent/reconciliation/dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253/`, `.agent/reconciliation/dogfood-2026-07-17-6d7a47b/`, `.agent/audits/audit-2026-07-17-cba6422/`) all exist on disk; item 4's six cited NDEBT ids (013-018) are all present in docs/planning/DEBT.md; item 6's claim ('14 rows, all Low or Medium') exactly matches a live Open-section-scoped severity sweep of the real DEBT.md -- 14 rows counted, all Low/Medium, zero High/Critical. Confirmed the contract's own non_goals ('No edit to docs/planning/DEBT.md ... this contract only READS them as evidence' and 'No remediation of any open ... DEBT.md item') textually forbid this contract's own implementation step from changing the Open section's row count or severities before item 6's pinned claim is checked, so the '14 rows' fact is correctly treated as immutable-during-this-contract's-own-execution, not a claim that could legitimately drift underneath the pin.

Probed the four-layer form (line-count, per-label-count, exact-line, SHA-256) for new vectors, including two the dispatch specifically flagged: (a) trailing whitespace appended to item 1's line -- correctly changes the SHA and fails; (b) CRLF line-ending variant of the whole file (identical rendered/visible content, different bytes) -- correctly changes the SHA and fails; (c) an extra trailing BLANK line appended after item 8 -- this is a genuinely useful finding about layer necessity, not a gap: `grep -c .` (layer 1) alone does NOT detect a trailing blank line (blank lines are excluded from its count, so the non-blank count stays 8), and layers 2/3 are similarly unaffected -- layers 1-3 ALONE would have let this fixture through. Layer 4 (the full-file SHA-256) is what actually catches it. This confirms the SHA-256 layer is NOT merely 'redundant-but-harmless' defense-in-depth as design_notes describes it, but the layer doing the real, necessary closing work for at least this one vector-class (trailing-blank-line insertion) that layers 1-3 alone would miss -- a point worth the Generator/future maintainers being aware of, but not a defect: the SHA-256 layer is present in entry 17 exactly as written and correctly closes it. No contradiction was found anywhere between the four layers (every fixture that fails one layer either fails identically at another layer or is correctly let through by all four only when it is byte-identical to the intended content).

Regression sweep confirmed no silent weakening: verification entries 4, 5, 7, and 11 (named in this round's dispatch) are byte-identical to their revision-1 text (direct comparison against this reviewer's round-1 record) -- the round-1/round-0 fixes to these entries were not touched or reverted in this revision. All other entries (1, 2, 3, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 19) are likewise byte-identical to revision 1, including entry 14's `before_count=10` hardcode (still an acknowledged, non-blocking observation, not required to fix). scope.files_modify (the 5 authorized files), non_goals (13 items), and estimated_lines (116) are unchanged; scope.files_create[0]'s purpose was expanded to carry the pinned exact byte content, which is the specification clarification the round-1 fix required, not scope growth. Prepare-only invariants remain textually intact and are independently re-confirmed live: real repository tag count is still exactly 10 (matching entry 14's pinned before_count), no sign-off was simulated, and CHANGELOG.md/CONTEXT.md/NIZAM.json/README.md/docs/guide/index.html/docs/planning/DEBT.md were read-only inspected (never mutated) throughout this round.

Probe-isolation compliance (per this round's explicit reminder, following the prior round's self-disclosed incident): cwd was verified as the evaluator's own scratchpad directory (045review/) before any file-writing or fixture-construction command in this round; no destructive command (rm/mv/redirect) was issued against any real-repository-rooted path at any point in this round. Confirmed clean at the end of the session: `git status --porcelain` on the real repository shows only the expected untracked proposal artifacts (.agent/contracts/045.json, .agent/qa/045-contract-review.json, the pre-existing v0.6.0-release-notes.md), and `git diff --stat HEAD` against CHANGELOG.md/CONTEXT.md/NIZAM.json/README.md/docs/guide/index.html/docs/planning/DEBT.md is empty (fully clean, no real-repo mutation this round).

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 2. The round-1 HIGH finding (entry 17's checklist whole-file shape was unbounded, allowing an adjacent line, a duplicate contradictory line-7, or trailing free-text prose to smuggle an implied sign-off/tag-complete state past the gate) is CONFIRMED GENUINELY CLOSED: all three of this reviewer's own round-1 kill-shot fixtures, re-run verbatim against the revision-2 entry-17 command, now correctly fail (each on the new exactly-8-non-blank-lines check); the round-0 same-line-smuggled-prose kill-shot still correctly fails on the retained exact-line check. The new full-file SHA-256 pin was independently verified achievable, not unsatisfiable: the exact 8-line content extracted programmatically from scope.files_create[0].purpose hashes to precisely the pinned value `06f266c06abd9b3793477771de265698dbec0a00761c6d81818decbc7e31b5ea`, and this content's items 7/8 are byte-identical to the retained `grep -qxF` literals. Content-honesty of the pinned items 1-6 was independently re-verified against the real, current repository (compliance-sweep output, dogfood/audit directory existence, DEBT.md NDEBT-013..018 registration, and the DEBT.md Open-section '14 rows, all Low/Medium' claim all match live reality exactly), and the contract's own non_goals confirm no scope in this same contract could legitimately alter that DEBT.md fact before implementation completes. A probe of the four-layer form for new vectors found none material: trailing-whitespace and CRLF variants are correctly rejected by the SHA-256 layer, and a trailing-blank-line variant (which layers 1-3 alone would have missed) is correctly caught by the SHA-256 layer -- confirming the layering is sound and the SHA-256 layer is doing genuinely necessary closing work, not merely redundant defense-in-depth. Regression sweep confirms entries 4, 5, 7, 11, and all other previously-fixed/unflagged entries are byte-unchanged from revision 1, with no silent weakening of scope, non_goals, or estimated_lines, and all prepare-only invariants (no tag creation, no self-approval, no run_state.json edit) remain textually and operationally intact. This contract is fit for implementation as-is. next_state: advance to Loop-2 (@generator implements the contracted scope; @validator Mode B verifies the implementation against this approved contract; this evaluator then runs the post-code QA verdict).
